body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
irrlicht_observes: n/a

## Reasoning

The spec describes a continuous self-prompting mode where the agent picks its own next steps without user prompts between internal iterations — the canonical examples cited are Codex `/goal`, Plandex full-auto, SWE-agent, and Goose recipes.

Claude Code does not ship an equivalent autonomous-loop mode. The interactive TUI is strictly turn-driven: each user prompt produces exactly one assistant turn that ends with either a final assistant message (→ `ready`) or a tool-permission prompt (→ `waiting`). The CLI exposes no `/goal`, no `--auto`, no recipe runner, and no headless equivalent that loops on a self-generated objective. `claude -p` (headless) is single-shot — it prints one response and exits. The closest user-visible loop construct is the manual `claude --resume` cycle, but that still requires the human to advance each turn.

Because the agent does not produce the behaviour, there is nothing for irrlicht's parser to observe. The 'no `ready` between internal iterations' invariant the spec asserts only makes sense against an agent that actually self-prompts, which claudecode does not. The matrix cell is correctly recorded as `agent_supports: no` and consequently `irrlicht_observes: n/a` — there is no transport judgement to make until the underlying capability exists.

## What would change this verdict

If Anthropic ships an autonomous-loop primitive (e.g. an `--auto` flag or a `Task`-driven loop visible to the top-level session rather than to subagents), this cell would re-open. The parser would need to verify that internal iterations stay on the same JSONL transcript (no session_birth) and that no `working → ready → working` flicker appears between iterations. Today neither is testable.
caveats: Claude Code's `Task` subagent tool does perform multi-step work without user prompts, but each Task is scoped as a child session with its own ParentSessionID — that is the foreground-subagent scenario, not an autonomous loop on the parent. The parent session still alternates `working`/`ready` per user turn.
Plan mode (Shift+Tab) is also not an autonomous loop: it gates execution behind an explicit human approve/edit step before the agent proceeds — that's the plan-mode approval scenario.

sources.note: ExactName{Name: "claude"} — the adapter watches the single interactive process; there is no autonomous-loop entry point distinct from a normal claudecode session.
Feature: Autonomous loop (self-prompting) at line 160 cites Codex `/goal`, Plandex, SWE-agent, Goose — explicitly NOT claudecode.
No `--auto`, no `/goal`-equivalent slash command, no recipe runner. Headless mode (`-p`) is single-shot.
